reviewers.name: A — Peer SME (Math PhD)
notes: All worked computations verified: y=3e^(2x) solves dy/dx=2y; dy/dx=x/y separates to y²=x²+C (hyperbolic family); dy/dx=2xy with y(0)=5 gives y=5e^(x²); bacterial doubling gives k=ln(2)/4.
Slope-field example dy/dx=x/y is well chosen — the family lines up with the separation derivation on the next slide, giving narrative continuity.
BC-only material (Euler's Method, logistic) correctly excluded.

reviewers.name: B — Adversarial Student

issues.description: The 'wrong' column logic is a bit muddled — needs tightening. Should clearly say: WRONG = plugging in IC before integrating (or before +C is written), so the constant has nowhere to absorb the IC. RIGHT = integrate → +C → exponentiate (A absorbs) → THEN plug in IC. Fix in writer stage by simplifying the compare lines.

notes: Sec 09 'only works when factors' is fine for AB scope; full theory is BC-territory.
Sec 12 absorbing ±A into a positive A is standard AB convention — AP rubrics accept this.

reviewers.name: C — Citation Checker (CED Unit 7)
notes: BC-only FUN 7.5 (Euler's Method) and FUN 7.9 (Logistic) correctly excluded.
All three CED-listed common traps addressed (forgetting +C, separation algebra pitfalls, slope-field direction misreading).

aggregate.rationale: 0 critical + 1 minor → approve outline. The single minor (sec 11 wording) is a tightening note resolvable at writer stage; no re-review needed.
